title: AB 522 Defends Independent Contractors by Repealing AB-5, Protects Small Businesses from Frivolous Lawsuits, and Imposes Transparency and Accountability on Labor Unions
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem
raw_preview:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 522 repeals AB 5 (2019), allowing businesses to hire independent contractors without classifying them as employees.",
    "AB 522 aims to stop frivolous lawsuits generated under the Private Attorney Generals Act (PAGA) that have negatively impacted small businesses.",
raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 522 repeals AB 5 (2019), allowing businesses to hire independent contractors without classifying them as employees.",
    "AB 522 aims to stop frivolous lawsuits generated under the Private Attorney Generals Act (PAGA) that have negatively impacted small businesses.",
    "The bill is designed to restore flexibility for Californians to work on their terms without unnecessary regulatory barriers.",
    "AB 522 aims to reduce the resources drained from small businesses due to costly and unnecessary legal battles.",
    "The bill promotes the creation of job opportunities by empowering small businesses.",
    "AB 522 places greater transparency and accountability requirements on labor unions concerning their use of member funds.",
    "Under AB 522, employee organizations must notify members of their membership costs annually."
  ]
}
```
